name: Feynman Mode rejects empty and too-short explanations with a clear error
description: A logged-in user opens Feynman Mode and attempts to submit an explanation that is either empty or fewer than 50 characters. The Server Action enforces a 50-character minimum. The test verifies that the app shows a human-readable validation error on screen — not a crash, a blank page, or a silent no-op — before any AI call is made.

planSteps.description: Log in with valid credentials and wait for the dashboard to finish loading
If a topic selector is shown, select any available topic so the explanation input becomes active
Click the explanation text area without typing anything, then click the submit / evaluate button
Verify a validation error message appears on screen (e.g. 'Please write an explanation first' or similar) — no crash, no blank screen, no spinner that never resolves
Type a very short explanation of exactly 10 characters (e.g. 'too short.') into the explanation field and click submit again
Verify the app again shows a validation error message rejecting the too-short input — the error text should reference a minimum length or ask for a more detailed explanation
